assessment.title: The Burnout Check-In
assessment.subtitle: A 3-minute honest look at where you actually are.
assessment.intro: This is not a test. There are no wrong answers. Answer from the body, not the mind — the first response is usually the truest. This assessment is inspired by clinical burnout research but not a diagnostic tool. If you are in crisis, please speak with a qualified professional.

emotional_exhaustion.name: Emotional Exhaustion
emotional_exhaustion.description: The wrung-out feeling. Nothing left to give.

cynicism.name: Cynicism & Disconnection
cynicism.description: Caring less. Going through the motions.

reduced_accomplishment.name: Reduced Sense of Accomplishment
reduced_accomplishment.description: Doubting your effectiveness. Wins don't land.

questions.text: By the end of the day, I feel completely used up.
I wake up already tired — like I never actually rested.
Small requests feel disproportionately heavy.
I have nothing left for the people I love when I'm done with work.
I feel like I'm running on fumes most of the time.
I've become more detached from things I used to care about.
I find myself being sharper or more impatient with people than I want to be.
The thought of another week feels crushing before it even starts.
I feel like I'm just going through the motions.
I've stopped looking forward to things I used to enjoy.
I question whether what I do actually matters.
When I finish something difficult, I don't feel any real satisfaction.
I feel less competent than I used to, even at things I know well.
It's hard to focus for long stretches the way I once could.
I second-guess decisions I would've made quickly a year ago.
I notice tension in my shoulders, neck, jaw, or chest most days.
My sleep is broken — I wake in the night or wake unrested.
My digestion feels off — bloating, appetite shifts, or stomach tightness.
I get more frequent headaches, sickness, or low-grade aches than I used to.
My breath often feels shallow — like it doesn't reach the bottom of my lungs.

scoring.method: Sum all responses, weighting somatic questions by 1.2. Maximum weighted score: 84.
scoring.calculation: For each answer (0-4), sum them. Multiply the sum of somatic (q16-q20) by 1.2. Final score = sum(q1-q15) + 1.2 * sum(q16-q20).

tiers.title: You're holding the line — let's keep it that way.
tiers.message: You're navigating stress, but your system is still resourced. A short reset will top up the reserves before they deplete. Prevention is easier than recovery.

supporting: One evening chakra session per week

tiers.title: You're deeper in than you've been letting yourself admit.
tiers.message: The signs are here. Exhaustion, disconnection, and your body is starting to speak up. This is a critical window — ignored now, it compounds. Caught now, it reverses. The 21-Day Journey was designed for exactly this moment.

supporting: Commit to daily practice for 21 days

tiers.title: Your system is asking for a real pause. Listen.
tiers.message: You are running on depletion — not willpower. This level of burnout does not heal through doing more. It heals through structured nervous system restoration, and often, a second set of eyes. The 21-Day Journey plus a 1:1 session will give you both.

supporting: Prioritize sleep and movement above all else
Reach out to one trusted human today

tiers.title: Please read this slowly.
tiers.message: Your answers indicate a level of exhaustion and disconnection that deserves professional care. Burnout at this stage can mask — or coexist with — depression, anxiety disorders, or thyroid and hormonal issues. We strongly encourage you to speak with a doctor or qualified mental health professional this week. Use the gentle daily practices in this app as support, not as a replacement for care.

supporting: Contact your GP or a therapist this week
Use only the shortest, gentlest practices for now (Box Breath, Deep Rest)
Tell one person in your life what you just read

safetyDisclaimer: This assessment is a self-reflection tool, not a clinical diagnosis. If you are experiencing thoughts of self-harm, overwhelming hopelessness, or are unable to function in daily life, please contact a qualified mental health professional or emergency services immediately.
